title: A fresh look is coming to our Welcome to Bathurst signs
content:
Home
City News
A fresh look is coming to our Welcome to Bathurst signs
A fresh look is coming to our Welcome to Bathurst signs
April 27, 2026
Starting tomorrow, you may notice our Welcome to Bathurst signs being worked on around the city.
As part of our rebrand, these signs are being refreshed to better reflect the City’s new look and offer an even warmer welcome to residents and visitors alike.
Our Public Works team will be working on refreshing the signs, and they’ll be back soon with an updated look.
Thanks for your patience as we make these improvements!

title: Newly renovated, fully accessible washrooms at La Promenade Waterfront
content:
Home
City News
Newly renovated, fully accessible washrooms at La Promenade Waterfront
Newly renovated, fully accessible washrooms at La Promenade Waterfront
April 24, 2026
The public washrooms at
La Promenade Waterfront
have been fully renovated, with accessibility at the centre of the redesign.
The updated facilities include:
Full wheelchair accessibility throughout
Automatic doors at the entrance
Large, easy-to-press buttons to operate the doors
Whether you're out shopping, walking the boardwalk, catching the sunset, or just out for some fresh air, the renovated washrooms are open and ready for you.
Next time you're visiting La Promenade Waterfront, take a look — and help us spread the word.

title: Jeux de l'Acadie 2028 Bathurst - Bid to host
content:
Home
City News
Jeux de l'Acadie 2028 Bathurst - Bid to host
Jeux de l'Acadie 2028 Bathurst - Bid to host
April 22, 2026
Bathurst is bidding to host the 2028 Jeux de l'Acadie — and as a City, we're behind this project 100%.
The Jeux de l'Acadie are the largest celebration of Acadian youth in Atlantic Canada. Over 6 days, a single host community welcomes more than 1,200 athletes and artists, 300 coaches, 9 regions from the 4 Atlantic provinces, and close to 30,000 visitors. Picture what that means for Bathurst.
Whether you're a volunteer, an athlete, a sports fan, or just someone who wants to see our local economy grow — there's something in this for all of us.
The official bid is submitted on May 1, 2026. Until then, every show of support counts. Like the page, follow it, share it with the people around you. The more our region shows up united, the stronger our bid becomes.
Jeux de l'Acadie 2028 Bathurst - Mise en candidature
Let's bring the Games home.

title: Public Notice – 2321 Rough Waters Drive
content:
Home
City News
Public Notice – 2321 Rough Waters Drive
Public Notice – 2321 Rough Waters Drive
April 21, 2026
Notice is hereby given that Bathurst City Council intends to consider amendments to By-Law No. 2006-01 entitled "The City of Bathurst Municipal Plan By-Law” and to By-Law 2008-01 entitled “The City of Bathurst Zoning By-Law” as follow:
Subject: Rezoning 2321 Rough Waters Drive
Change zoning of lot bearing civic numbers 2321 Rough Waters Drive and bearing PID 20571790, 20571808, 20194809 and 20036612. For the purposes of the municipal plan, the land use designation is changed from “Rural” to “Industrial”. For the purpose of the zoning by-law, the zoning is changed from “Rural–RU Zone” to “Light Industrial – LI Zone”.
The purpose of this rezoning is to allow the establishment and operation of a wood pallet production and distribution business
Interested parties may examine the proposed amendments at the Bathurst City Hall at the address mentioned below from Monday to Friday between the hours of 9:00 a.m. and 3:30 p.m. Written objections must be received by the City Clerk by 4:30 p.m. on Wednesday, June 10, 2026.
Written objections to the proposed amendments will be considered at a meeting which will be held in the Council Chambers of the City of Bathurst on June 15, 2026, at 6:30 p.m. Anyone wishing to defend or oppose these objections can do so at this time and place.
-
City Clerk
City of Bathurst
150 St-George Street
Bathurst, NB E2A 1B5

clerk@bathurst.ca

title: Strategic objectives met as Bathurst records 2025 surplus
content:
Home
City News
Strategic objectives met as Bathurst records 2025 surplus
Strategic objectives met as Bathurst records 2025 surplus
April 20, 2026
Bathurst completed its 2025 financial exercise with a surplus of just over $1 million dollars. The audited financial statements for 2025 were presented to City Council at its regular public meeting on Monday evening.
The 2025 General Fund (operational) presented a $706,418 surplus, while the Utility Fund (Water and Sewer services) also recorded a $ 343,562 surplus. Combined, this makes for an overall surplus of $1,049,981 for the 2025 fiscal year.
“The results show that the initial objectives identified during the 2025 budget were met, including having operations where we maximize efficiencies, optimize opportunities, while being sustainable. The results reflect judicious decisions on behalf of all departments”, said Treasurer Sonia Gauvin.
The city continued on its sound fiscal management efforts while accomplishing many strategic objectives:
Housing Development: $1.6 M in incentives were invested, double the budgeted $780,000, generating $366,00 in building permit revenues and creating more accessible housing (Housing Accelerator Fund);
Healthy Community: The city acquired the Bathurst Aquatic Center and now has fully consolidated its operations, enabling support from various departments. The Tourism Accommodation Levy collected $289k, with $133k disbursed as tourism grants and $154k invested in new initiatives;
Transportation: The Flex Go transit services began their operations in January 2026, with preparations done in 2025. In addition, a grant of $332k was provided to the Bathurst Regional Airport in support of its revitalization efforts, securing continued air passenger services to Montreal with Propair;
Corporate Branding: To reflect Bathurst’s new boundaries, a full branding exercise was budgeted. Most of the work was done in 2025, with the launch in March 2026;
Capital Investments: Continued increase in investments where many new investments are paid cash ($3.13M) out of a total of $3.8M. $561k were also transferred to the reserve to enable additional capital cash investments within the 2026 budget;
Stabilizing the Utility Fund: After incurring deficits for many years, the utility fund is in surplus position in 2025. The rates are also reflective of an operational breakeven. The surplus shown is related to unexpected additional revenues from Belle-Baie matched with accounting year end entries. This means the rates are reflective of the true operational cost for 2025 and is a starting point for a stable utility fund.
GENERAL FUND
Statements show expenses were below budgeted amounts in several areas, with savings in salaries ($218,779), snow and ice removal ($550,949), various fuels ($150,646), human resources ($190,765), insurance ($54,813), waste management ($56,403), travel ($52,469), training ($41,232), and other various savings of $ 165,066.
Revenue increases over budgeted amounts were recorded with the Housing Accelerator Fund ($831,597), the Aquatic Center ($578,858), the K.C. Irving Regional Centre ($250,003), 911 services ($182,419), building permits ($65,838), Worksafe refunds ($89,919), The Tourism Accommodation Levy ($39,432), police training and operations ($121,048).
UTILITY FUND
There were noted financial improvements in Utility operations in 2025, namely with $459,768 in additional revenues. With additional expenses of $115,830, the Utility Fund had a $343,830 surplus.
CONTINUED DEBT PAYMENT
The City reduced its net debt by another $ 2,041,691 in 2025. This makes for total debt reduction of $21.4 million or 57% since 2017, from $37,795,089 to $16,428,543.
“We commend all our personnel for their all-around performance, whether it be enhanced approaches in our day-to-day operations, constant efforts in efficiencies and budget mindfulness. There is a positive culture towards continued financial and operational improvement within the corporation, one that is strongly felt. Every contribution is a building block for the City’s positive standing, and we thank our staff for their outstanding efforts” said Mayor Kim Chamberlain.

Notice is hereby given that Bathurst City Council intends to consider amendments to By-Law No. 2006-01 entitled "The City of Bathurst Municipal Plan By-Law” as follow:

title: Charges laid
content:
Home
City News
Charges laid
Charges laid
April 07, 2026
On May 26, 2025, the Bathurst Police Force received a complaint involving one of its members alleging a possible criminal offence.
On May 27th, 2025, Bathurst Police Force notified the Serious Incident Response Team (SiRT), which assumed responsibility for the investigation as the matter fell within its mandate.
The officer was subsequently placed on administrative leave, pending the outcome of the investigation.
Following the conclusion of this investigation, on April 7th 2026, Constable Nathan Pitre has been charged in Provincial Court with the following criminal charges:
Criminal harassment
Harassment by telecommunication
Breach of trust
Unlawfully in a dwelling
Uttering threats
As this matter is now before the courts, we will not be providing any further details or commenting on this matter.
The Bathurst Police Force reaffirms its commitment to integrity and transparency and continues to apply rigorous mechanisms whenever serious allegations involve one of its members.
SIRT is responsible for investigating all matters that involve death, serious injury, sexual assault and intimate partner violence or other matters of a public interest that may have arisen from the actions of a police officer, on or off duty, in Nova Scotia or New Brunswick. Investigations are under the direction and control of an independent civilian director, who has the sole authority to determine if charges should be laid at the conclusion of an investigation.
Anyone with information is asked to contact SIRT by calling 1-855-450-2010 or via email at
sirt@novascotia.ca
.

title: Bathurst selected as one of Atlantic Canada’s top employers
content:
Home
City News
Bathurst selected as one of Atlantic Canada’s top employers
Bathurst selected as one of Atlantic Canada’s top employers
March 24, 2026
The City of Bathurst was announced as one of Atlantic Canada’s top employers. Organized by the editors of Canada's Top 100 Employers, the competition evaluates employers based on eight criteria, including workplace, benefits, training, and community involvement.
“Over the past five years, we have dedicated our efforts to enhance our workplace culture, and the synergy within our various departments to maximize the positive impacts. I take this opportunity to recognize these efforts and the pride of our employees to be the best they can be, while receiving the best support for individual and collective growth. We are proud of this recognition, and it encourages us to only reach for higher goals and constantly improve”, said C.A.O. Todd Pettigrew.
Bathurst is the only New Brunswick municipality to receive the Top Atlantic Employer recognition in 2026 and is one of only 17 entities in New Brunswick and 60 in Atlantic Canada.
The sustained growth, diversification and development of Bathurst have been a long adventure, since the municipality’s incorporation as a city in 1966, this year marking the city’s 60th anniversary.
“Many people have called the corporation of the City of Bathurst their home away from home. They live here, work here and grow here. They have shown that by their efforts to constantly be the best of themselves. We’ve also supported their efforts with enhanced work-life balance opportunities. The result is a work environment we are collectively proud of. I congratulate our employees for their day-to-day efforts to be the best they can be, making this recognition one we can all be proud of”, said Mayor Kim Chamberlain.

title: Register with Voyent Alert!
content:
Home
Communications
Communications
Register with Voyent Alert!
Voyent Alert! Mass Notification
Introducing Voyent Alert!
In times of crisis it is important that residents have access to trusted, timely and accurate information to ensure their own safety and that of their family and loved ones. In response to this need, the City of Bathurst has chosen
Voyent Alert!
as the communication service provider for these kinds of events.
Voyent Alert! is a multi-purpose communication service used to send alerts to residents, businesses, and visitors during critical events like fires or floods as well as for relevant day-to-day communications such as snow removal advisories, planned maintenance, water advisories, etc.
How to register:
Registration for the service is
FREE
, simple and totally anonymous.
For Mobile App Alerts: Download and install the Voyent Alert! app from the
Apple
or
Google Play
app stores.
For Email, Text Message or Voice Call Alerts: Register online at
https://register.voyent-alert.com
What you can expect:
Personalized Communications:
Voyent Alert! provides informative communications. Critical information such as the distance and direction from an incident, time of intercept and preferred evacuation routes from your followed locations are provided.
Follow Multiple Locations
: Voyent Alert! allows you to create and follow multiple locations such as “Kid's School” or “Mom’s House”. Any event or communication related to your followed locations will be forwarded to you along your preferred communication channel.
No Message Fatigue:
Voyent Alert’s smart alerting capabilities ensure that you will only get notified when a communication is relevant to you or one of the locations you are following.
Communications Your Way:
Receive alerts over a wide variety of communication channels including mobile apps, text/SMS alerting or email. You can register for all or one of them. (NOTE - Voice calling will be available in the near future).
Privacy is Paramount:
Registration is anonymous, and no information volunteered or derived is shared or used for marketing or data harvesting purposes. Locational information from your followed locations is only used to determine its proximity to an alert event and to provide critical context within the communications (such as distance and direction).
Leaving is Easy:
Both mobile applications and web-based accounts provide access to an unsubscribe feature accessed via the menu icon on the top right of the screen/page. If the service isn’t working out simply click on the “Unsubscribe” button and you won’t be hearing from us again.
For more information you can check out the
FAQ
and the
Voyent Alert Privacy Policy
.

content:
Home
City News
Subject: Rezoning 1005 St. Anne Street
Subject: Rezoning 1005 St. Anne Street
February 18, 2026
Notice is hereby given that Bathurst City Council intends to consider amendments to By-Law No. 2006-01 entitled "The City of Bathurst Municipal Plan By-Law” and to By-Law 2008-01 entitled “The City of Bathurst Zoning By-Law” as follow:
Subject: Rezoning 1005 St. Anne Street
Change the zoning of lot bearing civic numbers 1005 St. Anne Street and bearing PID 20759379. For the purposes of the municipal plan, the land use designation is changed from “Commercial” to “Integrated Development”. For the purpose of the zoning by-law, the zoning is changed from “Highway Commercial – HC Zone” to “Integrated development – ID Zone”.
The purpose of this rezoning is to allow the development of a residential complex comprising several multi-unit rowhouses.
Interested parties may examine the proposed amendments at the Bathurst City Hall at the address mentioned below from Monday to Friday between the hours of 9:00 a.m. and 3:30 p.m. Written objections must be received by the City Clerk by 4:30 p.m. on Thursday, April 2nd, 2026.
Written objections to the proposed amendments will be considered at a meeting which will be held in the Council Chambers of the City of Bathurst on April 7, 2026, at 6:30 p.m. Anyone wishing to defend or oppose these objections can do so at this time and place.

title: Bridge Street + Miramichi Avenue Reconstruction Begins May 18
content:
Home
City News
Bridge Street + Miramichi Avenue Reconstruction Begins May 18
Bridge Street + Miramichi Avenue Reconstruction Begins May 18
May 15, 2026
A major infrastructure rebuild gets underway — here’s what residents need to know.
Reconstruction work on Bridge Street and Miramichi Avenue will begin on May 18, 2026. The project covers a 1.1-kilometre stretch from the Bridge Street/Main Street intersection, across the bridge, and along Miramichi Avenue to approximately Couture Street. It includes a full rebuild of the road, water, and sewer infrastructure, with work continuing through the end of 2026.
Water Quality — May 19–21
A valve shutoff is currently scheduled for Wednesday, May 20. This may cause temporary water discolouration for residents in East Bathurst, Downtown Bathurst, and South Bathurst.
If your water appears discoloured:
Run cold water only for 15 to 20 minutes, or until the water has cleared.
Monitor your water quality periodically on May 19, 20, and 21 as the project ramps up.
Check your water before doing laundry on any of those days.
Traffic Impacts
Traffic disruptions are expected throughout the duration of the project. Residents and motorists are asked to follow posted signage and to exercise caution around active work zones.
Localized Boil Water Advisories
Residents near the work area should be aware that localized boil water advisories may be required at various points during the project. When this happens, notices will be delivered directly to affected properties by the project contractor or the contractor’s on-site representatives.
These localized notices will not be sent through Voyent Alert! or other City communication channels. This process ensures that residents directly affected by the work receive accurate, timely information specific to their property or area.
The City thanks residents for their patience and cooperation as this important infrastructure investment gets underway.

title: Register with Voyent Alert!
content:
Home
Communications
Communications
Register with Voyent Alert!
Voyent Alert! Mass Notification
Introducing Voyent Alert!
In times of crisis it is important that residents have access to trusted, timely and accurate information to ensure their own safety and that of their family and loved ones. In response to this need, the City of Bathurst has chosen
Voyent Alert!
as the communication service provider for these kinds of events.
Voyent Alert! is a multi-purpose communication service used to send alerts to residents, businesses, and visitors during critical events like fires or floods as well as for relevant day-to-day communications such as snow removal advisories, planned maintenance, water advisories, etc.
How to register:
Registration for the service is
FREE
, simple and totally anonymous.
For Mobile App Alerts: Download and install the Voyent Alert! app from the
Apple
or
Google Play
app stores.
For Email, Text Message or Voice Call Alerts: Register online at
https://register.voyent-alert.com
What you can expect:
Personalized Communications:
Voyent Alert! provides informative communications. Critical information such as the distance and direction from an incident, time of intercept and preferred evacuation routes from your followed locations are provided.
Follow Multiple Locations
: Voyent Alert! allows you to create and follow multiple locations such as “Kid's School” or “Mom’s House”. Any event or communication related to your followed locations will be forwarded to you along your preferred communication channel.
No Message Fatigue:
Voyent Alert’s smart alerting capabilities ensure that you will only get notified when a communication is relevant to you or one of the locations you are following.
Communications Your Way:
Receive alerts over a wide variety of communication channels including mobile apps, text/SMS alerting or email. You can register for all or one of them. (NOTE - Voice calling will be available in the near future).
Privacy is Paramount:
Registration is anonymous, and no information volunteered or derived is shared or used for marketing or data harvesting purposes. Locational information from your followed locations is only used to determine its proximity to an alert event and to provide critical context within the communications (such as distance and direction).
Leaving is Easy:
Both mobile applications and web-based accounts provide access to an unsubscribe feature accessed via the menu icon on the top right of the screen/page. If the service isn’t working out simply click on the “Unsubscribe” button and you won’t be hearing from us again.
For more information you can check out the
FAQ
and the
Voyent Alert Privacy Policy
.

title: Fire destroys East Bathurst home
content:
Home
City News
Fire destroys East Bathurst home
Fire destroys East Bathurst home
May 18, 2026
On Sunday May 17th at approximately 3:30pm, the Bathurst Fire Department was activated to a residential structure fire at 1080 Bayview Court, in East Bathurst.
Upon arrival, fire crews noticed thick smoke emanating from the roof of the structure. Crews were also confronted with heavy flames on the backside of the structure. While activating water suppression, firefighters accessed the structure and noticed the attic was also impacted by flames.
With high winds generating concern, a team from the Ste Anne Fire Department was called in to assist in flame suppression and to protect adjacent structures. Both fire teams were able to rapidly control and extinguish the fire. Unfortunately, the house was burned beyond repair.
No one was inside the structure at the time of the fire. However, two pets were caught in the blaze, one perishing.
A preliminary investigation was conducted into the cause of the blaze as well as a follow-up investigation by the Office of the Provincial Fire Marshal, the results yet to be confirmed.

title: Free Disc Golf Workshop
content:
Home
City News
Free Disc Golf Workshop
Free Disc Golf Workshop
May 22, 2026
Free Disc Golf Workshop at Coronation Park
Date: June 6, 2026
Time: 10 AM – 12 PM (approx.)
Location: Coronation Park, Bathurst (meet at the park entrance near the playgrounds)
Whether you're brand new to the game or just curious to try it, join us for a fun and friendly Intro to Disc Golf Workshop led by a Disc Golf Pro from Miramichi.
We’ll cover the basics of disc golf before heading out for a casual round on the course together. No experience is necessary, and all equipment will be provided free of charge during the workshop.
Free to participate
Discs provided
No registration required — just show up!
Want to keep playing after the workshop? Free disc golf rental kits are always available at the K.C. Irving Regional Centre during open hours.
Planning your own round? Check out our interactive course walkthrough at:
www.bathurst.ca/disc-golf
Come give it a try — we hope to see you there!
